title: Verilator does not accept module port lists with .port_identifier
body:
IEEE Std 1354-2001 Version C gives the following BNF:
```
port ::=
  [ port expression ]
  | .port_identifier([port_expression])
```
And p.187 includes the example
```
module same_port (.a(i), .b(i)); // Name 'i' is declared inside the
                    // module as a inout port. Names 'a' and 'b' are
                    // defined for port connections.
inout i;
wire i;
endmodule
```
where I've added the last three lines to complete the story.
Feeding that to verilator v5.030 yields
```
$ verilator --lint-only same_port.v
%Error: same_port.v:1:19: syntax error, unexpected '.', expecting '['
    1 | module same_port (.a(i), .b(i));
      |                   ^
%Error: same_port.v:6:1: syntax error, unexpected endmodule
    4 | endmodule
      | ^~~~~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
```
Vivado and iverilog both handle this case correctly.
